description_long: This object is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared brass horn mounted atop a wooden base. The horn is wide and conical, with a slightly weathered, matte finish, and is attached to the side of the base by a metallic bracket. The base is rectangular, made of dark-stained wood, and supported by four decorative columns at each corner, giving it a classic, antique appearance. On the top surface of the base sits a circular turntable platter, likely made of metal, with a visible tonearm extending over it. The tonearm is metallic and includes a small, rounded end where the stylus would be located. There are no visible control knobs or dust cover, and the overall design is ornate and reminiscent of early 20th-century phonographs.
description: A vintage gramophone-style record player with a large brass horn and a wooden base, featuring a metal turntable and tonearm. The design is ornate and antique, with no visible control knobs or dust cover.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide opening of the brass horn and the wooden base with decorative columns.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length and curvature of the brass horn, as well as the metallic tonearm and turntable platter.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the narrow end of the horn and the back of the wooden base, with the tonearm extending outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horn's attachment bracket and the full length of the tonearm over the platter.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and metallic, occupying a central position on the wooden base. It appears to be made of steel or a similar metal, with a slightly raised edge and a smooth, matte finish. The platter is medium-sized relative to the base, with no visible grooves or decorative patterns.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and extends from the side of the base over the turntable platter. It is straight with a rounded end, likely where the stylus is attached. The tonearm is silver in color and appears to be resting over the platter.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but the end of the tonearm suggests its likely presence as a small, dark component at the tip.
